返回本批次

三百个合成器、3 个硬件项目和 1 个应用程序

Three hundred synths, 3 hardware projects, and one app

midi.guide 作者 ductionist 分数 114 评论 17 2026/04/07 13:12
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. MIDI Guide，开放的、“综合的”MIDI CC & NRPN 数据集，已经远远超出了它最初的目的。 2026 年 4 月 6 日 下载黑暗：PNG、SVG、PDF；光：PNG、SVG、PDF。最近，我合并了一位首次贡献者的工作，将 MIDI Guide 的数据集放入了超过 300 种乐器。拉取请求是对我从未听说过的合成器（L.L. Electronics 的 RozzBox One V2）的 MIDI CC 实现（在我看来有点特殊）的完整参考，这是 MIDI Guide 社区向我介绍的另一个很酷的合成器。三百件乐器！还有 52 位贡献者，几乎全都是陌生人。并且至少有 3 个现实生活中的硬件设备在使用该数据集（它是 CC-BY-SA 4.0，所以也可以在您的项目中随意使用它）。就在添加第 300 个定义之前，我们发布了 MIDI Guide 的起源应用程序：Condukt，这是我们适用于 iOS、iPad 和 macOS 的性能 MIDI 控制器和音序器。但2026年发布的应用程序如何在2019年产生社区项目呢？以下是一个被搁置了七年的热情项目如何催生出一些有用的东西，然后最终启动的故事。故事是如何开始的 2019 年初，我和我的联合创始人从五年来的“folie à deux”中醒来，我们遗憾地开发了 Windows 软件。是时候掸掉 iPad 上的灰尘，尝试与真正的 App Store 中的巨头竞争了。在考虑构建什么时，我们重新发现了购买甚至使用合成器的乐趣。那是一个令人兴奋的开始囤积合成器的时刻：#dawless 标签承诺即使没有电脑也能提高生产力和幸福感，而 OP-1 重新发行版则被指责为业余爱好者的玩具，其售价高达 1,299 美元，滑稽颓废。值得注意的是，Eurorack 仍然位于实验室内，尚未在城市废水监测中被观察到。我们为“市场研究”购买的第一批合成器之一是 Elektron Analog Four MKII。它可能仍然是我最喜欢的合成器，因为它不仅仅是一个合成器：凭借其音序器和四个轨道，我可以在没有任何其他设备的情况下制作一个像样的即兴演奏。但特别让我们着迷的是它的表演模式。当您完成创作后，A4 会将旋钮映射到多个可自定义的参数。一触即变。我们想，如果 iPad 应用程序能够同时为多个合成器做到这一点，那不是很好吗？我们将 Condukt 设想为一个单一的应用程序，就像整个工作室的 A4 性能旋钮一样。我们建造了它！无论如何，它的测试版。这是在 iPadOS 12 上运行的实际效果：
  2. 对于几个生疏的人来说，创建应用程序本身就是一个可预见的挑战，但它表明即使使用我们的应用程序也是一个障碍。系统要求很高：
  3. 一个现代的iPad...和（可能）一个Lightning-to-USB适配器...和一个USB-to-MIDI适配器...以及一种为所有这些东西供电的方法...当然还有一个合成器...以及一个MIDI CC（和NRPN）数据库，以便应用程序可以与合成器对话。
  4. 由于所有的警告，这个东西根本不是一个有市场的应用程序。对于客户来说，拥有 iPad 是不够的（与 iPhone 相比，市场已经很小）——他们还需要适配器、电缆……那么商业方面呢？我们喜欢使用该应用程序，但无法分发它。至于货币化，应用商店刚刚开始认真对待订阅，而音乐应用程序的客户并不喜欢它。所以我们决定搁置Condukt。我们整个夏天都在研究 Penbook——现在是 App Store 编辑精选——但是如何处理我已经记录的 MIDI CC 实现呢？嗯，这并不是说 MIDI 文档变坏了；而是。我使用 40 年前的扫描为这个项目添加了一些合成器。说实话，我对我们为记录 MIDI CC 和 NRPN 而设计的格式有点自负。我认为这种形式很好地实现了两个经常对立的目标：
  5. 机器可解析 规范可读

Original Summary

  1. MIDI Guide, the open, "comprehensive" MIDI CC & NRPN dataset, has far outgrown its original purpose. April 6, 2026 Download dark: PNG, SVG, PDF; light: PNG, SVG, PDF. Recently, I merged in the work of a first-time contributor, putting MIDI Guide's dataset over 300 instruments. The pull request was a complete reference for the (somewhat idiosyncratic, in my opinion) MIDI CC implementation of a synth I'd never heard of, the RozzBox One V2 by L.L. Electronics - yet another cool synth introduced to me by MIDI Guide's community. Three hundred instruments! And from 52 contributors, too, almost all of them perfect strangers. And there are at least 3 real-life hardware devices using the dataset (it's CC-BY-SA 4.0, so feel free to use it in your project, too). Just before adding the three hundredth definition, we released the app that was the genesis of MIDI Guide: Condukt, our performance MIDI controller and sequencer for iOS, iPad, and macOS. But how did an app released in 2026 produce a community project in 2019? Here's how a passion project that was backburnered for seven years spawned something useful, and then finally launched. How it started In early 2019, my cofounder and I awoke from a half-decade folie à deux in which we regrettably made Windows software. It was time to dust off our iPads and try to compete with the big dogs in the real App Store. And while considering what to build, we rediscovered the joy of purchasing, and even using, synthesizers. It was a heady time to start a synth hoard: the #dawless tag promised productivity and happiness, even without a computer, and the OP-1 reissue was denounced as a toy for dilettantes at the hilariously-decadent price of US$1,299. Significantly, Eurorack was still contained in a lab, and had not yet been observed in metropolitan wastewater surveillance. One of our first synth purchases for our 'market research' was the Elektron Analog Four MKII. It's probably still my favourite synth, because it's so much more than a synth: with its sequencer and four tracks, I can make a decent jam without any other device. But what especially fascinated us was its performance mode. When you're done composing, the A4 maps knobs to multiple, customizable parameters. One touch, many changes. Wouldn't it be nice, we thought, if an iPad app could do that, for multiple synths at once? We envisioned Condukt as a single app that would act like the A4's performance knobs for your entire studio. And we built it! A beta of it, anyway. Here it is in action, running on iPadOS 12:
  2. Creating the app was its own predictable challenge for a couple of rusty carpetbaggers, but it revealed that even using our app was a hurdle. The system requirements were steep:
  3. a modern iPad... and (likely) a Lightning-to-USB adapter... and a USB-to-MIDI adapter... and a way to power all these things... and a synth, of course... and a database of MIDI CCs (and NRPNs) so the app could talk to the synths.
  4. Because of all the caveats, this thing was simply not a marketable app. It wasn't enough for customers to own an iPad (already a small market compared to iPhone) - they also needed dongles, cables... And what about the business side? We liked playing with the app, but had no way to get distribution for it. As for monetization, the App Store was just then getting serious about subscriptions, and music app customers were NOT having it. So we resolved to set Condukt aside. We spent the summer working on Penbook, instead - now an App Store Editor's Choice - but what to do with the MIDI CC implementations I had already documented? Well, it's not like MIDI documentation goes bad; I've used 40-year-old scans to add some synths to this project. And to be honest, I was a bit vain about the format we designed for documenting MIDI CCs and NRPNs. I thought the format did a decent job of meeting two oft-opposing goals:
  5. Be machine-parsable Be normie-readable

评论（默认中文）

Lio 2026/04/07 17:04
这看起来真是一个很棒的项目。

我天真地认为，有了 300 多个合成器，他们就会拥有我拥有的一切，但我发现事实并非如此。

我有 Alesis、Casio 和 Yamaha 设备丢失。 是时候找出手册并准备好 PR 了。

人们很容易忘记 MIDI 标准是多么成功。 它可能是有史以来最稳定且仍然相关的数字标准。

我最古老的套件是卡西欧 CZ-5000，我想是 1985 年的。我可以将它插入最新的设备而无需驱动程序，而且它仍然可以工作，这真是太神奇了。 5 针 DIN 获胜！

This looks like a really great project.

I naively thought that with 300ish synths covered they'd have everything I own but I can see that's not the case.

I've got Alesis, Casio and Yamaha equipment that's missing. Time to dig out the manuals and get a PR ready.

It's easy to forget how successful the MIDI standard is. It might be the most stable and still relevant digital standard of all time.

My oldest bit of kit is a Casio CZ-5000 from, I think, 1985. That I can plug it into the latest equipment without drivers and it still works is amazing. 5 pin DIN for the win!

在 HN 中查看
ductionist 2026/04/07 22:36
嘿谢谢。我也正是因为这个原因喜欢 MIDI 标准。令我震惊的是，你可以将四十年前的合成器连接到计算机或 iPad 上，而无需驱动程序。
Hey thanks. I love the MIDI standard for exactly this reason too. Blows my mind that you can hook a forty year old synth up to a computer or iPad without drivers.
在 HN 中查看
rigonkulous 2026/04/07 23:16
合成器迷们的做法是对的：开放的规格，以及整个行业都希望让一切能够很好地协同工作。 毕竟，它是音乐，这就是音乐发挥作用的首要原因。
Synth nerds got it right: open specs, and a general industry-wide desire to make things play well together. After all, its music, this is why music works in the first place..
在 HN 中查看
ductionist 2026/04/07 23:22
总而言之，我认为对 MIDI 指南的需求 - 事实上 MIDI CC/NRPN 几乎是免费的 - 也是该规范具有如此持久力的原因。它是如此不拘一格，以至于除了消息大小之外，它基本上施加了零限制。我喜欢它。
Totally, and I think the need for MIDI Guide - the fact that MIDI CC/NRPN is pretty much a free-for-all - is also why the spec has such staying power. It's so unopinionated that it imposes essentially zero constraints beyond message size. I love it.
在 HN 中查看
jamesjolliffe 2026/04/07 20:24
好吧，你也可以称我为合成器书呆子，但是你们是否曾经渴望过一个可以让您在合成器之间匹配信封时间等内容的项目？

例如。 （本例中的值完全是虚构的）如果您想近似从 SH-101 到 Bass Station 2 的幅度包络，如果起音旋钮位于 101 上的 5/10 位置，则为 500ms，这意味着您需要将 Bass Station 2 上的起音旋钮设置为 6/10 以获得相同的起音时间？

我希望有一天能做到这一点，但我太穷了，太愚蠢了，做不到。

无论如何，这种系统将使创建在合成器之间工作的“通用”补丁变得更加容易。

OK, call me too synth nerdy, but have you guys ever longed for a project that allowed you too match stuff like envelope times and between synths?

E.g. (totally made up values in this example) if you want to approximate the amplitude envelope from SH-101 to Bass Station 2, if the attack knob is at 5/10 position on 101, that's 500ms, which means you need to set attack knob to 6/10 on Bass Station 2 to get same attack time?

I hope this gets made one day, but I'm too poor and stupid to make it.

Anyways, this sort of system would make it much easier to create "universal" patches that would work between synths.

在 HN 中查看
ductionist 2026/04/07 22:42
这是一个有趣的想法。我们收到了一些扩展格式的请求，以包含 CC/NRPN 值和“显示”值之间的映射函数，例如[0, 127] -> [-10, 10]，适用于非线性关系的情况。这不能保证意义的标准化（它不会对 SH-101 的攻击和 Bass Station 2 的攻击之间的差异进行编码），但它可以更轻松地将所有内容整合到应用程序中。
That's an interesting idea. We've had a couple of requests to expand the format to include a mapping function between the CC/NRPN values and the 'display' values, e.g. [0, 127] -> [-10, 10], for cases where the relationship is nonlinear. This wouldn't guarantee normalization of meaning (it wouldn't encode the difference between the SH-101's attack and the Bass Station 2's attack), but it would make it easier to pull everything together in an app.
在 HN 中查看
bzzzt 2026/04/07 21:04
我不确定通过映射值可以获得很多好处。也许包络时间是可比的，因为秒就是秒，但并非每个合成器都以相同的方式插值 ADSR 值（有些只使用线性或对数插值，一些高端合成器是高度可配置的）。此外，其他值（例如滤波器截止）会以截然不同的方式影响声音，具体取决于滤波器的类型......
I'm not sure there's much to be gained by mapping values. Maybe envelope time is comparable since seconds are seconds, but not every synth interpolates ADSR values in the same way (some use just linear or logarithmic interpolation, some high end synths are heavily configurable). Also, other values like filter cutoff impact the sound in dramatically different ways depending on the kind of filter...
在 HN 中查看
jamesjolliffe 2026/04/07 21:15
好点 - 同样，滤波器截止旋钮位置或 0/最大值 # 的 MIDI CC 值百分比将映射到截止频率 Hz #。

是的，包络时间在点之间有不同的转换公式，如线性与对数，如您所述。但同样，拥有一个仅 A、D、S、R 毫秒时间的直接映射将为您提供 amp env 的粗略近似值，为您节省大量时间，而且说实话，当尝试在合成器之间重新创建类似的“经典”减法补丁时，有时只是作为健全性检查。

Good point - again for this, filter cutoff knob position or MIDI CC value percentage of 0/max value # would be mapped to cutoff frequency Hz #.

And yes, envelope times have different transition formulas between points, like linear vs logarithmic, as you stated. But again, having a direct map for of just A,D,S,R ms times would give you a rough approximation of amp env, save you a ton of time, and honestly, just serve as a sanity check sometimes, when trying to recreate similar "classic" subtractive patches between synths.

在 HN 中查看
ErroneousBosh 2026/04/07 21:03
> 好吧，也称我为合成器书呆子，但是你们是否曾经渴望过一个项目，让您也可以匹配包络时间和合成器之间的东西？

是的，实际上。我考虑过做类似的事情将 Juno 106 补丁转换为 Novation Xiosynth 补丁，因为我旁边有一个 106 和一个 Xiosynth 49，以及一个相当可行的 Juno 106 仿真。

获取 Juno 的时间很容易，因为查找表和信封的代码是已知数量，但我实际上只需要测量 Xio 中的信封。它们也可能不具有完全相同的响应“形状”，但它们会非常接近。

> OK, call me too synth nerdy, but have you guys ever longed for a project that allowed you too match stuff like envelope times and between synths?

Yes actually. I thought about doing something like this to convert Juno 106 patches to Novation Xiosynth patches, because I have a 106 and a Xiosynth 49 sitting beside me, and a pretty viable Juno 106 emulation.

Getting the times for the Juno is easy because the lookup tables and code for the envelope is a known quantity but I'd need to actually just measure the envelopes in the Xio. They might also not have quite the same response "shape" but they'd be pretty close.

在 HN 中查看
big_toast 2026/04/08 00:33
帖子中的演示 - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B8B4DuDFt2w

哇。这篇文章涵盖了很多！作为一个非合成器/MIDI 人，我有点反弹并认为这不适合我，但这里确实有很多很酷的东西。该视频促使我向后滚动并更仔细地阅读。

如果包含笔簿，它看起来就像两个应用程序（https://condukt.app/ 作为主要应用程序）。标题确实描述了内容，但不知何故对我来说低估了它。

有没有像小学生录音机一样新手入门的midi设备？ HiChord 看起来总是那么简单诱人，但我什至不知道这是否是同一类型的设备？

Demo from the post - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B8B4DuDFt2w

Wow. This post covers a lot! As a non-synth/midi person I kinda bounced off and thought it wasn't for me but there's really so much cool stuff in here. The video was what caused me to scroll back and read more closely.

It seems like two apps if you include penbook (https://condukt.app/ being the main app). The title really describes the content but somehow undersold it for me.

Is there a midi device novices start with like a recorder for gradeschool students? The HiChord always looks so enticingly simple but I don't even know if that is the same type of device?

在 HN 中查看
ductionist 2026/04/08 01:31
如果您对合成器感到好奇，我实际上会说最好从独立的乐器（即不需要 MIDI 控制器）开始。 HiChord 是一个不错的选择。我个人在 Telepathic Instruments 的 Orchid 中获得了很多乐趣。任何带有 KORG 键盘的产品（包括非常实惠的 volca 系列）也是一个很好的起点。

一旦您的第一个合成器开始有意义，您就会渴望购买第二个，并且是时候深入研究 MIDI 了:)

If you’re curious about synths, I’d actually say it’s better to start with an instrument that’s standalone (that is, doesn’t need a MIDI controller). The HiChord is a good choice. I’ve personally had lots of fun with Orchid by Telepathic Instruments. And anything with a keyboard by KORG (including the VERY affordable volca line) is also a great starting point.

Once your first synth starts making sense, you’ll get the itch to buy a second, and it’ll be time to delve into MIDI :)

在 HN 中查看
big_toast 2026/04/08 01:45
哈。当我试图回答自己的问题时，Orchid 是我点击的第一个乐器（在 Jamuary 部分）。

感谢您的建议。您已经到了 Logicalincrements.com 的一半（我假设所有这些设备之间存在自然配对？它看起来总是像一组通过电缆连接在一起的设备。）

Ha. The Orchid was the first instrument I clicked on (in the Jamuary section) when I was trying to answer my own question.

Thank you for the recommendations. You're half way to a logicalincrements.com (I assume there's natural pairings between all these devices? it always looks like an array of devices cabled together.)

Thanks! I have never heard of it. Need to buy my son a new ipad so I can use this with his old one.

Hah! I hope it's worth the expense. Check out the Condukt discord when you get it going, lots happening in there.

import 2026/04/07 16:57
非常酷的项目。 Hapax 等不同格式的下载能力给我留下了深刻的印象。荣誉！
Very cool project. I am impressed by the ability of the different format downloads like Hapax. Kudos!
在 HN 中查看
ductionist 2026/04/07 22:38
这是我最喜欢的功能:) 有很多很酷的音序器和控制器，它们都有自己独特的纯文本乐器定义格式。为他们每个人制作小出口商很有趣。
This is my pet feature :) There are so many cool sequencers and controllers, all with their own idiosyncratic plaintext instrument definition formats. Making little exporters for each of them is fun.
在 HN 中查看
marpstar 2026/04/08 01:35
很高兴在这里看到这个。我一直使用 iPad 作为我的音乐设置中的“大脑”，不久前发现了这个项目。此后，我为我拥有的几个 Line 6 踏板打开了 PR。我还有一个 Novation Launch Control XL 3，Ben（创建者）为其提供了一个编辑器，可以更轻松地定位特定设备。我和他有过几封关于开源该编辑器的想法的电子邮件，他是一个超级好的人。

再次感谢，本！ （如果你正在读这篇文章）

Glad to see this on here. I've been using an iPad as the "brain" in my music setup and found this project not long ago. I've since opened PRs for a couple of Line 6 pedals I've got. I've also got a Novation Launch Control XL 3, for which Ben (the creator) has made an editor available to make targeting specific devices much easier. I've had a few email correspondence with him regarding the idea of open-sourcing that editor and he's a super nice dude.

Thanks again, Ben! (if you're reading this)

在 HN 中查看
